questions: Is removing isHelixTagged: true the only CLI change needed?
Is it safe to deploy the CLI change before the server change?
Does the CLI need a --helix flag for external users?

answers.answer: Yes. The single change is removing isHelixTagged: true from the POST body at post.ts:33. The CLI sends only { content: message }. The server handles all attribution (isAgentAuthored, isHelixTagged) based on auth identity. No CLI flags, no local identity storage, no new commands needed.
evidence: post.ts:33 — body: { content: message, isHelixTagged: true } is the only line to change
product/product.md — 'CLI stops overriding Helix tagging: The CLI sends only the comment content'

answers.answer: Yes. If CLI deploys first (removing isHelixTagged: true), the server defaults isHelixTagged to false (comment-controller.ts:65-66) for non-agent comments. The server also forces isHelixTagged=true for isAgentAuthored comments (line 77). Since the old server treats all inspection auth as agent-authored, sandbox agent comments still get isHelixTagged=true. External CLI users' comments would correctly lose the Helix tag even before the server fix. No functional breakage.
evidence: comment-controller.ts:65-66 — isHelixTagged defaults to false when not in body
tech-research/tech-research.md — 'No functional breakage in either order'

answers.answer: Not for MVP. External CLI users who want to @mention Helix can use the web UI. The server does not parse @mentions from plain text. A CLI --helix flag is deferred to a future round per the tech-research decision.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — 'CLI --helix flag: Allow external CLI users to Helix-tag their comments from the command line' listed as Deferred to Round 2
product/product.md — Out of scope for MVP

statement: The CLI implementation plan is a single-line change: remove isHelixTagged: true from the POST body in post.ts. The server determines attribution based on auth identity. The change is safe to deploy in any order relative to the server fix. No new CLI flags or commands needed for MVP.
